description: WS-AP3915i / AP391x Qualcomm IPQ4029 access point — no-serial TFTP flash via rdwr_boot_cfg

hardware.flash: SPI-NOR 32MB (Macronix MX25L25635E, verified from DTS and hardware)
hardware.ethernet_ports: 1x GbE (GE1 only, PoE+ data — single cable carries power and data)

extreme-rdwr-tftp-initramfs.description: Two-stage no-serial flash from stock Extreme firmware. SSH to stock, write U-Boot variables using rdwr_boot_cfg, TFTP boot OpenWrt initramfs, backup flash, then sysupgrade to permanent OpenWrt.
extreme-rdwr-tftp-initramfs.note: run boot_flash is the STOCK boot command (source boot_kernel) — it does NOT work with OpenWrt FIT images. Use boot_openwrt instead.
extreme-rdwr-tftp-initramfs.note_final_uboot_vars: CRITICAL: bootcmd must be 'run boot_openwrt; run boot_net' (semicolon, NOT ||), NOT 'run boot_flash' or 'bootx'. boot_flash/bootx runs the stock boot_kernel script with watchdog/nboot/failover which is incompatible with OpenWrt FIT images. Semicolon provides TFTP fallback: if bootm succeeds it doesn't return (kernel boots), if bootm fails it returns and run boot_net catches via TFTP. See commit e16a0e7 and SESSION-WRITEUP.md. NOTE: Prefer writing U-Boot env from STOCK firmware (not initramfs) because the OpenWrt DTS marks CFG partitions as read-only. If writing from initramfs is required, kmod-mtd-rw IS available in 24.10.x: opkg update && opkg install kmod-mtd-rw && insmod mtd-rw i_want_a_brick=1.
extreme-rdwr-tftp-initramfs.fw_env_warning: fw_setenv does NOT work on this device — no /etc/fw_env.config for AP3915i in ipq40xx base-files. The AP3915i is missing from the uboot-envtools board list. Use rdwr_boot_cfg (from stock firmware), flashcp (from stock firmware), or raw MTD write to write U-Boot variables. MTD writes from OpenWrt initramfs require kmod-mtd-rw: opkg update && opkg install kmod-mtd-rw && insmod mtd-rw i_want_a_brick=1.

dts_partition_layout.note: OpenWrt DTS partition table (qcom-ipq4029-ws-ap3915i.dts). ALL partitions except 'firmware' are marked read-only. Stock firmware MTD indices differ from OpenWrt MTD indices.
dts_partition_layout.ap391x_warning: AP391x uses SPLIT firmware layout (firmware + firmware2, ~15MB each, IMAGE_SIZE=15040k). AP3915i uses MERGED layout (single firmware ~30MB, IMAGE_SIZE=30080k). Verify /proc/mtd firmware size from initramfs before sysupgrade.

sysupgrade.description: SSH sysupgrade from existing OpenWrt on this device

tftp.description: U-Boot TFTP recovery (requires serial console or rdwr_boot_cfg for network boot setup)
tftp.notes: No reset-button TFTP recovery. Use rdwr_boot_cfg to set bootcmd=run boot_net for TFTP boot, or use serial console to interrupt U-Boot.

hardware_variants.note: Two known hardware variants with identical SPI-NOR partition layout but different flash configurations.

spi_nor_only.description: Unit 1 — SPI-NOR only (32MB Macronix MX25L25635E), no NAND. U-Boot 2012.07.22. rdwr_boot_cfg BROKEN (exit 255). bootcmd defaults to 'run boot_flash'.

spi_nor_plus_nand.description: Unit 2 (AP3915i-ROW) — SPI-NOR + 512MB NAND (UBIFS). U-Boot 2012.07.19-r00020.1. rdwr_boot_cfg EXISTS but read_all returns empty. bootcmd defaults to 'bootx' (equivalent to 'run boot_flash'). NAND holds WiNG rootfs/config — irrelevant for OpenWrt which uses SPI-NOR only.

uboot_commands.bootx: Custom QCA command — equivalent to 'run boot_flash'. Runs source boot_kernel. DO NOT USE as final bootcmd with OpenWrt.
uboot_commands.boot_flash: source boot_kernel — runs stock boot script with watchdog/dual-image failover. Incompatible with OpenWrt FIT images.
uboot_commands.boot_net: tftpboot 0x83600000 vmlinux.gz.uImage.3912; bootm 0x83600000 — TFTP boot. Safe to use.
uboot_commands.boot_openwrt: sf probe; sf read 0x88000000 0x280000 0xc00000; bootm 0x88000000 — Direct SPI-NOR read of OpenWrt FIT. The CORRECT final boot command.
uboot_commands.bootipq: Custom QCA command — boot from flash device with device tree handling.

ssh.description: OpenWrt 24.10.2 on this device runs Dropbear 2025.89 SSH server. Several gotchas when configuring SSH from an OpenWrt host switch.
ssh.authorized_keys_note: NOT ~/.ssh/authorized_keys. OpenWrt Dropbear reads from /etc/dropbear/ only. conwrt already handles this correctly in scripts/conwrt.py (line 3589) and scripts/profile/builder.py (line 32).

key_format.problem: OpenWrt Dropbear dbclient cannot use OpenSSH-format private keys (error in libcrypto: unsupported). Use Dropbear-format keys or install openssh-client on the host.
key_format.workaround: opkg install openssh-client on the host OpenWrt device, or use dropbearkey to generate Dropbear-format keys.

password_env_var.description: dbclient accepts the password via DROPBEAR_PASSWORD env var for non-interactive auth. Example: DROPBEAR_PASSWORD=mypass dbclient root@192.168.1.1

algorithm_negotiation.problem: Dropbear 2025.89 on AP3915i may lack ssh-rsa hostkey. openssh-client from an OpenWrt host needs explicit algorithm enablement.

ordering.rule: Set SSH authorized_keys BEFORE setting root password.
ordering.reason: OpenWrt default allows passwordless root login. Once you set a password, passwordless login stops. If SSH keys aren't in place yet, you lose remote access.

cfg_block_format.description: U-Boot environment block format for AP391x devices (CFG1/CFG2 partitions, 64KB each).
cfg_block_format.crc_scope: CRC32 covers bytes[5:] to end of data (NOT including the CRC itself or the active flag byte). This differs from some U-Boot implementations where CRC covers byte[4:].

write_method.openwrt_initramfs: Requires kmod-mtd-rw (insmod mtd-rw.ko i_want_a_brick=1) to override DTS read-only flags, then flashcp or custom mtd_raw_write tool.
write_method.note: Always use flashcp for NOR flash, never dd. flashcp handles erase+write cycles correctly.

cfg_block_format.strategy: Write CFG1 only first, verify boot, then mirror to CFG2. Leaving CFG2 untouched provides a fallback if CFG1 is corrupted.

switch_initiated_flashing.description: Flash procedure orchestrated by an OpenWrt PoE switch (e.g. GS1900-8HP) — no laptop required.

poe_control.re_enable_quirk: After disabling and re-enabling PoE, the port may not power up immediately. Wait 5-10 seconds or toggle again. The BCM59121 PoE controller needs time to renegotiate.

network.secondary_ip: ip addr add 192.168.1.2/24 dev br-lan — add a secondary IP on the same interface for the flash subnet
network.note: dnsmasq doubles as TFTP server on OpenWrt (already installed for DNS/DHCP). No extra package needed.

workflow: 1. PoE disable target port (power off AP)
6. SSH to AP, sysupgrade with permanent image
7. Post-flash: set SSH keys, then set password, then change IP

port_isolation.description: When flashing from a DSA-capable OpenWrt switch (e.g. GS1900-8HP), the switch port can be isolated into a dedicated VLAN before powering on the AP. This prevents the AP's post-boot DHCP server from reaching other devices on the network.
port_isolation.switch_port_isolation_docs: See port_isolation section in models/zyxel-gs1900-8hp-a1.json for DSA topology, UCI commands, and gotchas.
workflow_with_isolation: 0. Switch isolates target port (lan5) into VLAN 999
1. PoE disable target port (power off AP)
2. Stage firmware + TFTP config (serve from 192.168.1.2 on br-lan.999)
3. PoE enable target port — AP boots in ISOLATED VLAN (DHCP offers contained)
4. AP U-Boot TFTP-boots initramfs (switch serves TFTP via br-lan.999)
5. SSH to AP (via br-lan.999), backup partitions
6. SSH to AP, sysupgrade with permanent image
7. Post-flash: set SSH keys, then set password, then change IP
8. Switch restores port to default VLAN — AP joins production network
port_isolation.note: Port isolation makes the overlay DHCP-disable approach (scripts/profile/overlay.py) unnecessary when flashing from a DSA switch. The overlay remains useful as a fallback when flashing from macOS or non-DSA-capable hosts.

extreme-rdwr-tftp-initramfs.notes: Switch-initiated flash via GS1900-8HP (OpenWrt 25.12.1). No laptop needed during flash. AP on switch port lan5 via PoE. rdwr_boot_cfg broken — used raw MTD write via kmod-mtd-rw + mtd_raw_write from initramfs. CFG blocks corrected with boot_openwrt command. Flash boot verified, overlay persisted through reboot. IP changed to 192.168.13.253 post-flash.

warnings: Misconfigured U-Boot environment may require serial recovery.
Stock Extreme shell may reboot periodically if no controller is present — disable SSH timeout with: cset sshtimeout 0 && capply && csave
Do not proceed without saving rdwr_boot_cfg read_all output.
Make a full MTD backup before permanent install.
Direct stock image_upgrade support is experimental unless signature/checksum status is proven.

CRITICAL: bootcmd must be 'run boot_openwrt; run boot_net' (semicolon) — NOT 'run boot_flash', NOT 'bootx', NOT '||'. boot_flash/bootx runs the stock boot_kernel script which is incompatible with OpenWrt FIT images. This mistake bricked Unit 1. Semicolon is safer than || because it doesn't depend on U-Boot operator support.
CRITICAL: Always include '; run boot_net' fallback in bootcmd. If flash boot fails, AP falls back to TFTP — permanent safety net.

CRITICAL: Write ALL U-Boot env changes from STOCK firmware, NOT from OpenWrt initramfs. The OpenWrt DTS marks CFG1/CFG2 as read-only. If writing from initramfs is required, install kmod-mtd-rw first: opkg update && opkg install kmod-mtd-rw && insmod mtd-rw i_want_a_brick=1. Stock firmware's /dev/mtd1 (CFG1) is writable via flashcp without any extra modules.
CRITICAL: Write CFG1 only (leave CFG2 untouched as fallback). rdwr_boot_cfg writes BOTH blocks — use flashcp instead if single-block write is needed. See REVIEW-UNIT2-FLASH-PLAN.md v2.
CRITICAL: Remove 'ubi.mtd=0' from bootargs if present — it causes kernel UBI attach failure on OpenWrt. The CFG block should not contain this parameter.
CRITICAL: Set SSH authorized_keys BEFORE setting root password. OpenWrt default allows passwordless root — once password is set, passwordless login stops.
CRITICAL: Do NOT change bootcmd from a working state. If the AP is booting correctly, do not rewrite the CFG blocks. Only write if bootcmd is wrong or AP is in a boot loop.
NOTE: Some units have NAND in addition to SPI-NOR. NAND is irrelevant for OpenWrt flashing — OpenWrt uses SPI-NOR only.
NOTE: Dropbear authorized_keys path is /etc/dropbear/authorized_keys, NOT ~/.ssh/authorized_keys.
NOTE: openssh-client on OpenWrt host cannot use Dropbear-format private keys. Use openssh-keygen or install openssh-client.
NOTE: After PoE disable/re-enable cycle, wait 5-10 seconds for the BCM59121 controller to renegotiate before expecting device boot.
